feedback.text: As many other people have already requested, it'd be great if this initiative could include protections for games and other digital goods. Currently, there are very few, if any, protections in place for the consumer in this regard. This allows companies to remove access to games when they deem it is not worth supporting it anymore. It doesn't matter if the game is online multiplayer only, or if it is a purely single player experience. Imagine that instead of a digital game, this was the case for physical games like Monopoly. The company could at any moment come and take the game away because they no longer feel like it's worth creating new copies or manage online resources for rules clarification. That wouldn't be accepted, so why do we accept that for digital games and other digital goods? Would that mean that the companies would be forced to continue supporting the games indefinitely? No. Hosting servers for online play and continuously updating games isn't free, and companies should be able to cut those costs if they feel like it's not worth it anymore. But, there should be ways for me, as a consumer, to still access the products I have paid for. How that is accomplished is less relevant and there is no one solution that will work for every single game, but it needs to be a requirement for companies to make the products we've paid for available to us as consumers. There is also the art preservation aspect of it, and while I know this isn't nearly as relevant for this initiative I still feel like it should be mentioned. If I buy a game, a movie, a song, or an art piece digitally and suddenly my access to that gets revoked, then that would lead to me not being able to preserve that piece of art for future generations. If my access to my favourite song got revoked for everyone, then I would not be able to show that song to my future kids. If my favourite movie was taken away, I wouldn't be able to share that experience with my partner. If access to the Mona Lisa got removed, we wouldn't be able to ponder her enigmatic expression. The same goes for games that are taken away in a similar fashion. We lose access to experiences that can have an incredible impact on both our lives and the lives of the people we care about.
